description_long: This helicopter features a lightweight, open-frame design with a visible tubular structure and exposed mechanical components, suggesting a utilitarian or experimental model. The main rotor consists of two long, slender blades mounted atop a central mast, while the tail boom extends rearward, supporting a small tail rotor for stability and control. The cockpit area is compact, with a rounded, transparent canopy providing visibility for the pilot, and the interior appears to have minimal instrumentation. The landing skids are simple, black, and tubular, running parallel beneath the fuselage and connected by crossbars for stability. Wiring and mechanical linkages are visible along the tail boom and undercarriage, indicating a functional, possibly remote-controlled or ultralight helicopter. The color scheme is predominantly black with some metallic and blue accents, and there are no visible registration markings.
description: A lightweight, open-frame helicopter with exposed mechanics, a rounded cockpit canopy, and black tubular landing skids. The design is utilitarian, with visible wiring and a predominantly black color scheme accented by metallic and blue details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, including the main rotor, tail boom, exposed cockpit, and landing skids.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail rotor, narrow tail boom, and the symmetry of the landing skids.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the cockpit, main rotor, and tail structure from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the rounded cockpit canopy, main rotor hub, and the parallel landing skids beneath.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow main rotor blades made of carbon fiber or composite material, colored black with no visible markings, each blade approximately the length of the helicopter's main body.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small, two-bladed tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored black with no visible markings or protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Simple, black tubular landing skids with a rectangular profile, connected by crossbars for stability, made of metal or carbon fiber.
attribute_cockpit_windows: One large, rounded, transparent cockpit canopy forming the front and sides of the cockpit, with minimal framing and no visible tint or markings.
